specific_mechanism: Mercator's cylindrical projection preserves angle locally (conformal) but inflates area by sec(φ) where φ is latitude; at φ→90° the metric blows up. Cartographers correct visual area by overlaying Tissot indicatrices or by switching to equal-area substitutes (Mollweide, Gall-Peters). The 'lost information' in Mercator is the cosine-weighting needed to recover spherical area.
llm_application: Treat dot-product attention's softmax score as a planar (Mercator-like) projection of the underlying spherical-manifold similarity between query and key vectors. At high-norm regions (the 'poles' of the embedding sphere) the planar score over-amplifies, analogous to Mercator inflation. Apply a learned cos(angular-latitude) weighting to attention logits at high-norm tokens so the effective similarity respects spherical area, then ablate the contribution of polar-region tokens via null-space projection during inference.
novelty_claim: While spherical attention (Attention on the Sphere 2505.11157, SphereUFormer CVPR 2025) integrates quadrature weights for spherical data domains, none specifically frames the high-norm-token inflation in standard transformer attention as a cartographic distortion and prescribes a Mercator-correction cosine weight applied at inference. The framing as a null-space-traversal that removes 'polar' contributions is the open novelty question.
